description_long: This object consists of six cylindrical green tubes arranged in a circular cluster, each tube held together by a series of dark, thin wires or bands that wrap around the group. The tubes are mounted on a single central shaft or handle, which is metallic and has a threaded end at the bottom. The green tubes have closed tops and slightly flared rims, giving them a robust, industrial appearance. The wires or bands are evenly spaced and provide structural integrity to the cluster, suggesting a purpose-built design. The overall construction and coloration imply a military or paramilitary application, with the green paint likely serving as camouflage.
description: A cluster of six green cylindrical tubes bound by dark wires, mounted on a central metallic shaft with a threaded end, resembling a military-style bomb.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular arrangement of six green tubes and the central shaft with visible wire wrappings.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the closed tops of the tubes and the even spacing of the wires around the cluster.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetry of the tube arrangement and the central shaft extending downward.
description_view_3: Another angled view emphasizing the flared rims of the tubes and the consistent wire pattern.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No explicit detonation mechanism is visible, but the presence of wires suggests a possible electrical or remote triggering system.
